  1. Install all necessary project dependencies:

npm install

== 6. Previewing Your CAP App Locally ==

To see how your app behaves locally, start the development server:

cds watch

The terminal will display a URL where your CAP service is accessible. Click the link to open a preview interface, which features a basic UI generated using Fiori Elements.

== 7. Adding a Managed Application Router ==

  1. Right-click on the mta.yaml file and select Create MTA Module from Template.

  2. Choose the Approuter Configuration template.

  3. Follow the wizard:

    • Use a Managed Approuter.

    • Name it samplerouter or another descriptive identifier.

    • Indicate that a Fiori UI will be added later.

  4. Finish the wizard to generate the approuter configuration, including a xs-security.json file.

== 8. Adding XSUAA for Authentication and Authorization ==

XSUAA is required for deploying CAP apps to Cloud Foundry with authentication support.

cds add xsuaa --for production
npm install

This updates your package.json and adds authentication requirements under the "requires" section.

== 9. Creating a SAP Fiori UI App ==

  1. Right-click mta.yaml → Create MTA Module from Template.

  2. Choose SAP Fiori Application and select List Report Page as the template.

  3. In the Data Source step:

    • Select Local CAP Project

    • Pick the bookshop project

    • Choose the CatalogService (Node.js)

  4. Confirm Books as the main entity.

  5. Set module name (e.g., booksui), title, and namespace.

  6. Enable deployment options and FLP configuration.

  7. For deployment:

  8. Target: Cloud Foundry

  9.  

    • Destination: Local CAP Project API

    • Semantic Object: bookshop

    • Action: show

    • Title: Books

Finish the wizard to generate the UI module.

== 10. Deploying the Application to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry ==

10.1 Login to Cloud Foundry

Use the command palette:

CTRL + Shift + P → CF: Login to Cloud Foundry

Provide the API endpoint, credentials, and select the correct organization and space.

10.2 Bind the HANA HDI Container

  1. Open the SAP HANA Projects panel in BAS.

          Bind to a HDI Container

         Select "+ Create a new service instance" 

  2. Click bookshop-db → Bind → Create new service instance.

  3. Name it bookshop-db and enable auto undeploy.

10.3 Build and Deploy the MTA Archive

 

Run the following in your terminal:

mbt build
cf deploy mta_archives/bookshop_1.0.0.mtar

This creates and deploys your application archive.

== 11. Verifying Deployment and Launching Your Fiori App ==

  1. In the BTP cockpit, go to Services → Instances. You should see instances for the db, approuter, CAP service, and xsuaa.

  2. Go to HTML5 Applications (enabled via SAP Build Work Zone) and find your booksui app.

  3. Launch the app to see the same Fiori UI you previewed locally, now hosted on the cloud.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Application Generator Available in Open Source
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori tools team are delighted to announce that the underlying code for the SAP Fiori Application generator has been moved entirely to open source. This follows in our vision that was first outlined by our Chief Architect in a blog post from 2021 (Yes, this has been a long journey ‌🙂‌). 

Why Open Source?

Our goals haven’t changed—only expanded: 

  • Tighter collaboration. Work side-by-side with the community rather than behind closed doors. 
  • Full transparency. See exactly how the generator works, suggest improvements, or fork it for your own needs. 
  • Faster innovation & broader adoption. Community pull-requests beat private roadmaps every time. 

Our open source GitHub project now contains multiple packages that comprise the SAP Fiori Application Generator and span functionality from generation to deployment, including but not limited to: 

PackageWhat it does
@sap-ux/axios-extensionHandles authenticated HTTP calls so your generator prompts fetches remote data seamlessly. 

@SAP-ux/*-writer

Reusable file writers that output consistent and version-appropriate SAP Fiori application files for building SAP Fiori Elements and SAP Fiori Freestyle UI5 applications.
@sap-ux/deploy-toolingOpinionated deploy tasks for ABAP systems.
@sap-ux/odata-service-inquirer Provides reusable configurable inquirer prompts (for use in Yeoman generators) for connecting to multiple back-end types and retrieval of service data.
@sap-ux/fiori-app-sub-generator The main entry point for generation of SAP Fiori Elements based applications. Once a template type is selected in the @sap/generator-fiori generator this is the sub-generator that co-ordinates prompting the user, using the inquirers and generates the files, using the writers.

You’ll also find shared utilities, end-to-end test suites, and plenty of docs. 

 Image - SAP Fiori Tools and Generators - High-Level Modules and Dependencies

Not a simple "lift & shift"

Before open-sourcing the code, we broke up a monolith, added TypeScript typings, improved test coverage, and simplified internal APIs so you get clean, reusable, and well-documented code from day one.

How Can You help?

• Utilise our tools, try generating a SAP Fiori app, deploy it, and tell us where we can improve?
• Star & fork the repos of interest to raise their visibility.
• Open issues or PRs—bug fixes, new features, documentation tweaks.

Is there anything missing that you think should be there? Let us know - open a feature request at https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/issues.

entry.title: How To Implement Alert Notification Service in Cloud Application Programming Model (CAPM) Project
entry.content:
Step 1: Go to Subaccount -> Go To Instance and Subscription -> Click Create Button

Step 2: In Service Select Alert Notification and In the Instance Name Section Give Any Name, Leave Other Fields as It Is.

Note: If you did not create any space then you see create space option just below space filed. Just click on that give space name and choose roles and click Create

Step 3: Access SAP Alert Notification Cockpit and Create a Subscription

  1. After creating an instance, it will be listed under Instance and Subscription.

  2. Click on the instance name from the list.

  3. This will open the SAP Alert Notification Cockpit in a new page.

  4. In the cockpit, navigate to the Subscription section.

  5. Click the Create button to start creating a new subscription.

Step 4: Create Subscription with Condition and Action

  1. Create Subscription:

    • Go to the subscription creation section.

    • Enter a random name for the subscription.

    • Click Create to proceed to the next step.

  2. Create Condition:

    • Enter a random name for the condition.

    • In the Condition section, choose the criteria for triggering the email.

      • Example: Set eventType to Contains → Warning.

    • Click Create.

    • If multiple conditions are created, you can select the relevant one in the next step.

    • Click Save and Next to proceed.

  3. Create Action:

    • Select Email as the action type.

    • Click Next.

    • Provide:

      • Action Name

      • Email Address of the recipient.

      • Subject Template (for email header)

      • Payload Template (for email body)

    • Click Create.

    • On the next page, select the appropriate action and click Save.

Notes:

  • Note 1: You can create Actions and Conditions separately by navigating directly to the Action or Condition tabs from the left-side menu.

  • Note 2: The recipient email address will receive a confirmation email. They must accept it to start receiving notifications.

  • Note 3: To send Email Subject and Body dynamically from your application payload, leave the Subject Template and Payload Template fields blank.

Step 5: Create a Service Key for the Alert Notification Instance

  1. After setting up the Alert Notification instance, locate it in the list.

  2. Click on the arrow ( > ) next to the instance name.

  3. A panel will open on the right side of the screen.

  4. In the right panel, look for the Service Key section.

  5. Click the Create button.

  6. A popup window will appear for creating a new service key.

  7. Enter a name for the service key.

  8. Click Create to generate the key.

 

 

Step 6: Create Destination for Use in CAPM Application

  1. After creating the service key, the next step is to create a destination.

  2. This destination will be used in your CAPM application.

  3. To get the required details (URL, Client ID, Client Secret):

    • Click on the instance.

    • Select View Credentials to open the service key details.

    • Copy the URL, Client ID, and Client Secret from the credentials.

  4. In the left-side panel, go to Connectivity → Destinations.

  5. Click the Create Destination button.

  6. Fill in the destination details as shown in the reference screenshot:

    • URL

    • Authentication method

    • Client ID

    • Client Secret

    • Any other required fields

  7. Click Create to save the destination.

 

Step 7: Integrate Alert Notification in a CAPM Application

  1. Open or create a CAPM application where you want to integrate the alert notification.

  2. In the CAPM application, set up a connection to the destination you created earlier.

  3. Use the standard API endpoint to trigger the notification:

     
    /cf/producer/v1/resource-events

  4. This endpoint allows your application to send events to the SAP Alert Notification service.

  5. For full API details and supported parameters, refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 
 
 

Step 8: Test Using Postman

  1. Deploy your service.

  2. Use the deployed service URL.

  3. Get the Client ID and Client Secret from the environment variable under the xsuaa section and use it in postman for authentication using oAuth 2.0.

 
 

Conclusion:

By following the above steps, we have successfully set up and integrated SAP Alert Notification with a CAPM application. Starting from creating the alert notification instance, setting up conditions and actions, generating a service key, creating a destination, and finally integrating and testing the service using Postman — the entire process ensures that your application can send real-time alerts based on defined events. This integration enhances monitoring and improves responsiveness to critical events in your system.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q2 2025
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP continues to evolve and expand, offering our customers improved resources and streamlined access to product updates. As we step into Q2 2025, we are excited to announce the addition of 11 new product pages. Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Collaborating closely with SAP BTP experts, we've expanded the release coverage by introducing 11 new product pages. These pages compile links to all available release information, such as schedules, pre- and post-release communications, blogs, and more:

  • SAP Audit Log Service
  • SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service
  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment
  • SAP Connectivity Service
  • SAP Credential Store
  • SAP Custom Domain Service
  • SAP Destination Service
  • SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
  • SAP Malware Scanning Service
  • SAP Private Link Service
  • SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI

You will notice that several of these pages share certain resources which are relevant across SAP BTP services that are relating to security.

Here's a glimpse at our new additions:

 

1. SAP Audit Log Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Audit Log service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter
    • Customer Influence

Check it out.

 

2. SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Audit Log service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter
    • Customer Influence

Check it out. 

 

3. SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment

This page contains specific resources for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • Release Schedule in SAP Note
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer
  • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
  • SAP Community Tag
  • Documentation: SAP Help Portal

Check it out.

 

4. SAP Connectivity Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Connectivity service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Connectivity service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Connectivity service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center

For centralized information regarding the release of new features for SAP BTP products and services, consult the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP. Regarding the Release Navigator updates, refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer: The schedule is subject to alterations without notice, including potential delays or the integration of changes in subsequent update windows. It doesn't establish a commitment, promise, or legal duty to deliver any material, code, or functionality. Looking into the remaining quarters of 2025, updates will focus on quarterly content maintenance and a steady extension of product coverage. No substantial UI updates are planned for the time being.

 

The Readiness@Scale team within SAP Enterprise Support is driven to ensure release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to effectively adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have feedback or specific requests for content within the release navigator, feel free to contact us.

Don't forget to explore our team’s blogs tagged under Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale  
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: AI in Action: New mission available to enhance SAP Signavio with Machine Learning
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, organizations are continually seeking innovative ways to enhance operational efficiency and stay ahead of the competition. One groundbreaking approach is the integration of machine learning (ML) with process mining, a strategy that transforms static data into dynamic, predictive insights. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, in conjunction with SAP Build, offers a robust platform to achieve this integration seamlessly.

Unlocking Predictive Process Management

Traditionally, process mining involves analyzing historical event logs to understand past performance. While this retrospective analysis is valuable, it doesn't provide foresight into future outcomes. By embedding ML algorithms into your process mining activities, you can predict future events, identify potential bottlenecks before they occur, and make data-driven decisions that proactively enhance process efficiency.

Practical Applications

Consider the challenge of predicting sales order delivery times. By analyzing historical event logs, ML models can forecast delivery dates based on factors like order size, product type, and current workload. These predictions, when fed back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, offer real-time insights, enabling better planning and improved customer satisfaction.

Another application is in customer support. By examining event logs, ML can predict customer satisfaction levels with support tickets, considering variables such as resolution speed and communication quality. This allows support teams to proactively address issues, enhancing the overall customer experience.

New mission OUT NOW

We are excited to announce a new mission that showcases how you can realize these use cases using SAP Signavio in combination with SAP Build.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.signavio-pi-and-sap-build.html 

Successfully implement the mission and earn a cool badge as a reward.

 

It will highlight your success of combining multiple SAP solutions and showcase your ability to tackle new topics and realize value from it!

Seamless Integration with SAP Build

SAP Build, equipped with Python and Jupyter capabilities, serves as the bridge between raw data and actionable insights. It enables the extraction of event log data from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, the application of ML algorithms, and the reintegration of enriched data back into the system. This seamless workflow ensures that your process management system evolves from reactive to proactive, continually learning and adapting to new data.

Getting Started

Embarking on this transformative journey involves a few key steps:

  1. Activate APIs: Enable the OData and Ingestion APIs in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to facilitate data movement.
  2. Data Extraction: Utilize SAP Build to extract event log data via the OData API.
  3. Apply Machine Learning: Develop and train ML models within SAP Build to analyze and predict outcomes based on your data.
  4. Data Reintegration: Push the enriched, predictive data back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence through the Ingestion API.

    For a comprehensive, step-by-step guide on implementing this integration, refer to the mission above.

    By harnessing the combined power of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Build, your organization can transition to a predictive process management paradigm, driving efficiency, foresight, and a competitive edge in your industry.
